owner.description: engineer owns Worker source/test/migration files like src/**, workers/**, test/**, and migrations/**, plus product-specific config edits when source documents require them. In a brand-new Worker project, deterministic delivery-scaffold owns root rails such as package.json, .gitignore, wrangler.jsonc, tsconfig.json when needed, vitest.config.ts, the initial Worker entrypoint, and canonical test runtime routing before implementation agents run. Worker config tasks should define env.staging and env.production when they intentionally own product-specific config changes, mirroring required bindings and vars because Wrangler environments do not inherit them. New Worker package scripts should use wrangler dev --env staging and wrangler deploy --env production. TypeScript Worker scaffolds should use Wrangler-generated worker-configuration.d.ts types, not hand-written runtime types. designer owns static UI files like public/index.html, public/styles.css, public/app.js, and assets/**.

source_task_id.description: When a task is a generated or reviewed slice of a larger task, the original task id whose acceptance contract is being preserved.

source_acceptance_criteria.description: Original acceptance criteria carried forward when a task is split or revised. These are first-class contracts and must not disappear during planning or review.

owned_surfaces.description: Concrete repo paths this task will change, for example src/index.js, src/routes/tally.js, test/api-routes.test.ts, public/settings.html, migrations/0001_schema.sql, README.md, or product-specific config files. Wildcards such as src/**/*.ts, src/storage/*.ts, public/**, or src/** are not allowed. Use ['unknown: <reason>'] only when genuinely unknown; conceptual labels are not allowed. In a bare Worker folder, deterministic delivery-scaffold owns package.json, .gitignore, wrangler.jsonc, tsconfig.json when needed, vitest.config.ts, the initial Worker entrypoint, public shell files, contract shell files, and canonical Node/Worker/jsdom test runtime routing before implementation agents run. Plan product-specific changes to those files only when the source documents require implementation work there; Wrangler local validation remains wrangler dev --env staging and human-approved production deployment remains wrangler deploy --env production.

metadata.description: Optional workflow-derived metadata. The harness may add task.kind, surface.kind, evidence.kind, runtime.kind, and scaffold generated file facts after deterministic normalization; planners do not need to author this by hand.

open_decisions.description: Only genuine blockers. Each entry should use: Topic: ... | Why it matters: ... | Options considered: ... | Follow-up impact: ...
